title: Ruth Clark | Partner | BAL Immigration Law
content:
“Immigration law is compelling to me because it’s both intellectually challenging and emotionally fulfilling. I love that each day brings a new challenge that needs a creative solution, and I enjoy bringing clarity to the complexity of U.S. immigration law. Even more fulfilling are the relationships I have the opportunity to foster with our clients, their employees, as well as BAL’s talented team, and the impact I see my work have on their work and lives.”
Ruth is a Partner in BAL's Houston office. Ruth focuses her practice on corporate immigration matters related to temporary work visas, employment-based immigration, naturalization and consular processing. She represents multinational corporations in a variety of industries, including energy, technology and engineering. Ruth has extensive experience in developing strategies related to PERM labor certification. She also advises clients on I-9 program compliance and improvements, corporate immigration policies and the immigration implications of corporate restructuring, mergers and acquisitions. Ruth is a thought leader in corporate immigration, co-authoring articles and speaking to industry organizations. She is an avid runner and regularly competes in half-marathons.
University of Houston Law Center, J.D. University of Texas, B.A., French

### Think an I-9 investigation won’t happen to your company? Think again.
